critic_raw:
Assessor A: The central issue is metric-decision misalignment. The business uses exactly 2% of the scored population. AUC measures ranking quality across 100% of the population. These are measuring different things. The paper does not provide precision@2% or lift@2%, which are the only metrics that directly evaluate whether the model improvement matters at the actual decision boundary. Additionally, no retention outcome is reported for called users — the business goal is retention, not ranking. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor B: A 7-point AUC improvement is large, but it measures the wrong quantity for this use case. The 2% capacity constraint makes this a selective-intervention problem. Performance at the top 2% is what matters, and the paper doesn't report it. The team argues AUC is threshold-independent, but the operational reality is that the model will always be deployed at one threshold. Threshold-independence is irrelevant here. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed — compute top-2% metrics.

Assessor C: The churn model evaluation is incomplete for its stated purpose. It reports the global discrimination ability (AUC) but not the capacity-constrained selection quality (top-2% precision/lift) or the business outcome (incremental retention). These gaps make it impossible to assess whether the workflow switch is justified. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Synthesis: Three of three assessors identify the same three issues and unanimously vote empirical_test_agreed. The ETD is straightforward: compute capacity-constrained metrics from the existing test month data.
adjudication_raw: Full consensus across three assessors. All three identify the AUC-threshold misalignment, the missing capacity-constrained metrics, and the absent intervention outcome. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.measure: precision@2% and lift@2% on held-out test month
empirical_test.success_criterion: new model precision and lift at top-2% cutoff are materially higher than incumbent, with positive retention lift among called users
empirical_test.failure_criterion: top-2% precision and lift are not improved relative to incumbent despite AUC gain
